title: Max Verstappen fights back as George Russell delivers 'cold-blooded' display
summary:
Max Verstappen fought back from a big crash in qualifying to finish second in the Austrian Grand Prix, while George Russell converted pole position into a second win of 2026 at the Red Bull Ring.

Championship leader Kimi Antonelli completed the podium with just two seconds separating the top-three, in what became a strategic race at Red Bull's home circuit. It was a disappointing day for Ferrari, who lacked race pace after starting in second and third.

Join RacingNews365's Nick Golding and Samuel Coop as they dissect the Austrian GP!

title: Winner and losers from F1's blistering Austrian Grand Prix
summary:
Even the bubble that Formula 1 tends to operate in couldn't escape the latest European heatwave that has gripped the continent over the past week. The challenging conditions on Red Bull's home circuit served up an intriguing war of attrition, offering a different competitive picture compared to Barcelona which is leaving some frontrunners with more questions than answers.

summary:
Max Verstappen came as close as ever to his first win of the season in Austria. The Red Bull driver, having started fifth in Spielberg, emerged as George Russell's main pursuer during the Formula 1 Austrian Grand Prix and finished just over 1.6s behind.

summary:
Formula 1 championship leader Kimi Antonelli was left frustrated by his horrible start to the Austrian Grand Prix as he went off three times across the opening two laps.
The Mercedes driver started fourth and carried a lot of speed into Turn 1, forcing him over the sausage kerb and onto the escape road, with similar happening at Turn 3.

summary:

title: Red Bull apologises to Max Verstappen after technical issue caused F1 qualifying crash
summary:
During Q3 in F1 Austrian GP qualifying Max Verstappen lost control of his RB22 in Turn 9 at the Red Bull Ring after, according to his own account, already experiencing a significant snap three corners earlier.

title: Verstappen confused by car handling ahead of Q3 crash

title: Ferrari will need to pressure Mercedes via strategy in Austria - Hamilton
summary: “It’s going to be very tough to challenge them tomorrow, but with a long run down to Turn 3, hopefully together we can. It’s great having Charles [Leclerc] here as well, because we can hopefully work together in a strategy and try to apply pressure to them.”

title: Why Russell’s Austrian GP pole lap was legal
summary: When Max Verstappen lost control at Turn 9 on his final lap of Q3 at the Austrian Grand Prix, it seemed to lock in a Ferrari front row lock-out. But one key detail meant there were at least two cars that still had a chance to improve their lap time. And George Russell took that opportunity.

title: Why Antonelli aborted his fastest lap in Austrian GP qualifying
summary:
Qualifying for the Austrian Grand Prix ended in disarray after Max Verstappen crashed his Red Bull at the fast Turn 9 on his final push lap.
Championship leader Kimi Antonelli was also on a push lap, trying to improve on his previous time, and misinterpreted the waved yellow flags at the scene for double waved yellows.
Within the rulebook, the distinction between single and double waved ...Keep reading

title: Verstappen questions "crazy" delay in double yellow flags as the FIA responds
summary:
Max Verstappen believes his crash in one of the fastest sections at the Red Bull Ring should have triggered double yellow flags earlier, with the FIA explaining that the standard procedure was followed.
Verstappen lost control of his RB22 on the entry to Turn 9 after already experiencing a significant snap three corners earlier.
The rear of the Red Bull stepped out immediately on turn-in for ...Keep reading

title: Russell explains meaning behind Wolff's "just drive" radio message in Austrian GP qualifying
summary:
George Russell has explained Toto Wolff’s “just drive” radio message during Formula 1 Austrian Grand Prix that was a way to encourage the Briton who went on to secure pole position.

title: Leclerc slams Ferrari brake failure after Monaco GP crash
summary: On The Chequered Flag Podcast, BBC F1 correspondent Andrew Benson reads out Charles Leclerc's comments stating his Ferrari brake issue is "borderline dangerous" after crashing out of the Monaco Grand Prix. Running in a podium position late in the race, Leclerc lost control into the barriers after three out of four brakes failed to work.
